receiptQuality.summary: Execution receipts are incomplete for the selected workflow.

findings.evidence: The selected `ios-profiler-performance` lane requires XcodeBuildMCP build/run proof, but supplied receipts did not contain that signal.
findings.recommendation: Add XcodeBuildMCP build/run proof before claiming the selected LaunchDeck workflow has been executed and proven.
findings.proofGuidance: Re-run the relevant Codex/XcodeBuildMCP step, preserve the log/screenshot/trace output, then rerun `shipguard ios launchdeck --receipt <dir>`.

findings.evidence: The selected `ios-profiler-performance` lane requires profiler or performance capture proof, but supplied receipts did not contain that signal.
findings.recommendation: Add profiler or performance capture proof before claiming the selected LaunchDeck workflow has been executed and proven.
findings.proofGuidance: Re-run the relevant Codex/XcodeBuildMCP step, preserve the log/screenshot/trace output, then rerun `shipguard ios launchdeck --receipt <dir>`.

reportQualityQuestions: Does the report make ShipGuard feel like the front door for LaunchDeck rather than a separate plugin the user must remember?
Does it distinguish planned LaunchDeck routing from execution receipt quality after Codex runs the workflow?
When receipts are supplied, does it name missing build/run, UI, preview, log, or profiler proof for the selected lane?
